free_text: Determination of carbon and oxygen in GaAs
by means of 3He - activation
7 MV
30 minutes irradiation with 400 nanoAmp. beam
current.
Short and long counting times after the
irradiation offered the possibility of evaluating all
activities products.
The reaction products were identified and counted using
high resolution gamma-ray
Because of the successive
subtraction the overall error connected with the cross
section ranges from 10% to 20% depending on the
individual product nucleus and its gamma-ray intensity.
Therefore the errors given on figures represent only
the statistical errors.
Corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
REACTION code corrected in Subent 002.
New Subents inserted: .003-.011
The description was made by CAJAD and CNPD
Interchanged EN-ERR and EN-ERR1 headings;
moved EN-ERR from Subent 001 to relevant subentries.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
The accuracy of data presentation  has been revised.
Fig. 6 from Nucl.Instr.Meth.,B18(1987)281
Differentiation of thick target activity
Boron-10 implanted tantalum foils
Stat. errors connected with the count rate
Reaction code changed.
SD: SF9=EXP -> DERIV in REACTION code.
EN-ERR1 -> EN-ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG.
GaAs Crystals
Differentiation of thick target activity
Error bars of the determined excitation
functions do not represent the uncertainty of the beam
energy but rather indicate the energy region
corresponding to the determined mean cross section.
